Version
  • Theory
  • Energy Balance Equations
  • Energy Balance Equations

Energy Balance Equations

GreenhousesJL solves a system of ordinary differential equations (ODEs) representing the thermal energy balance of each node in the greenhouse.

Nodal temperatures

The model tracks 3 to 4 temperature nodes plus soil layers:

NodeSymbolThermal capacity
Cover (glass)$T_{cov}$$C_{cov} = \rho_{gl} \, c_{p,gl} \, h_{cov} \, A / \cos\varphi$
Indoor air$T_{air}$$C_{air} = \rho_{air} \, c_{p,air} \, A \, h_{air}$
Floor$T_{flr}$$C_{flr} = \rho_{flr} \, c_{p,flr} \, V_{flr}$
Walls (optional)$T_{wall}$$C_{wall} = \rho_{gl} \, c_{p,gl} \, h_{cov} \, A_{walls}$
Soil layers$T_{s,i}$$C_{s,i} = (\rho c_p)_i \, \Delta z_i \, A$

Cover balance

\[C_{cov} \frac{dT_{cov}}{dt} = \underbrace{Q_{cnv,air \to cov}}_{\text{interior convection}} + \underbrace{Q_{rad,flr \to cov}}_{\text{LW from floor}} + \underbrace{Q_{sol,cov}}_{\text{solar absorption}} - \underbrace{Q_{cnv,cov \to out}}_{\text{exterior convection}} - \underbrace{Q_{rad,cov \to sky}}_{\text{LW to sky}}\]

Air balance

\[C_{air} \frac{dT_{air}}{dt} = \underbrace{Q_{cnv,flr \to air}}_{\text{floor convection}} + \underbrace{Q_{heat}}_{\text{PI heater}} - \underbrace{Q_{cnv,air \to cov}}_{\text{cover convection}} - \underbrace{Q_{cnv,air \to wall}}_{\text{wall convection (if walls)}}\]

Floor balance

\[C_{flr} \frac{dT_{flr}}{dt} = \underbrace{Q_{sol,flr}}_{\text{solar absorption}} - \underbrace{Q_{cnv,flr \to air}}_{\text{convection to air}} - \underbrace{Q_{rad,flr \to cov}}_{\text{LW to cover}} - \underbrace{Q_{cd,flr \to soil}}_{\text{conduction to soil}}\]

Wall balance (optional)

\[C_{wall} \frac{dT_{wall}}{dt} = \underbrace{Q_{cnv,air \to wall}}_{\text{interior convection}} - \underbrace{Q_{cnv,wall \to out}}_{\text{exterior convection}} - \underbrace{Q_{rad,wall \to sky}}_{\text{LW to sky}}\]

PI heating controller

When heating is enabled ($K_p > 0$):

\[e(t) = T_{sp} - T_{air}(t)\]

\[Q_{heat}(t) = \text{clamp}\!\left( K_p \, e + \frac{K_p}{T_i} \int_0^t e \, d\tau ,\;\; 0 ,\;\; Q_{max} \right)\]

The integral $\int e \, d\tau$ is carried as an additional state variable in the ODE system, ensuring continuous tracking without discretisation error.
